title: WildFly Datasource Connection Pool Exhaustion
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`); file monitoring for `jboss:server`, structured audit for `wildfly:audit`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="jmx:jboss"
| search mbean="*data-source=*statistics*jdbc*"
| eval active=tonumber(ActiveCount) max=tonumber(MaxPoolSize)
| eval pct=if(max>0, round(100*active/max,1), null())
| where pct >= 95
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: JDBC pool saturation blocks servlet threads and causes cascading timeouts. JMX statistics expose active versus available connections without parsing stacks.
value: Speeds isolation of DB incidents from application code defects.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to speed isolation of DB incidents from application code defects.
implementation: Set `statistics-enabled=true` on datasource in `standalone.xml`. Ingest via `Splunk_TA_jmx`. Page when active/max ≥95%.
visualization: Time series for pool/queue metrics, top stats for failures, event timeline for deploys.
knownFalsePositives: Thread or worker pools fill during traffic spikes, slow upstream services, or DoS-style load. A surge alone can be benign if backends recover.
